title: SIP connections can be opened repeatedly without closing prior user agents
reasoning: connect always creates and starts a new UserAgent/Registerer and then overwrites uaRef/registererRef. If connect is called again while already registered, the previous user agent and registration are no longer referenced by the hook, so disconnect can only stop the newest connection. This can leak websocket/SIP resources and duplicate registrations.
reproduction: Call connect('1001','pw') twice without disconnecting. The second call overwrites uaRef/registererRef, leaving the first UserAgent/Registerer running outside the hook's cleanup path.
recommendation: Make connect idempotent or explicitly disconnect/unregister any existing user agent before creating a new one. Also guard concurrent connect calls so two in-flight connects cannot race and leak one of the instances.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are included for repeated connect calls or lifecycle cleanup.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a hook test that calls connect twice with mocked UserAgent/Registerer instances and asserts the first instance is unregistered/stopped or that the second call is ignored while already connected.
minimumFixScope: Update src/hooks/useSip.js connect/disconnect lifecycle handling to prevent replacing live UA/registerer refs without cleanup.
